home.guideSubtitle: Enjoy your eggs within 3–4 weeks of the lay dates for best quality!

batches.freshnessTip: Enjoy your eggs within 3-4 weeks of the lay dates for best quality!
batches.donationText: Suggested Batch Donation: $5 per dozen, or $4 if you return your carton!

meetFlockers.description: Get to know our wonderful backyard chickens! Each hen has her own personality and produces beautiful, fresh eggs.

chickenFax.description: Discover fascinating facts about our feathered friends and the amazing eggs they produce!

pricing.description: Pay what you can! All proceeds go toward chicken care and feeding.

chickens.name: Beyoncé
chickens.personality: Queen of the pecking order! Beyoncé is gentle with humans but commands respect within the flock.
chickens.eggProduction: 4-6 medium/large blue or green eggs weekly

chickens.name: Ke$ha
chickens.personality: An excellent forager who loves free-range time. She's a calm and beautiful breed with stunning speckled feathers.
chickens.eggProduction: 4-6 large light brown eggs per week

chickens.name: Cookie
chickens.personality: Our surprise addition! Cookie came as a complimentary chick with the original order.
chickens.eggProduction: Produces the smallest (and cutest!) eggs in the flock

chickens.name: RiRi
chickens.personality: Calm and friendly, representing Rhode Island's state bird!
chickens.eggProduction: Around 4-6 large brown eggs per week

chickens.name: Honey
chickens.personality: A heavy-breed hen who's calm and dependable.
chickens.eggProduction: Around 4-6 large brown eggs per week

chickens.name: Sunny
chickens.personality: Known for her friendly nature and cuddly appearance!

chickens.name: Sugar
chickens.personality: Sweet and friendly, loves human interaction!

funFacts.title: Impressive Egg Production
funFacts.content: Hens produce approximately 250-300 eggs per year, depending on the breed. That's almost one egg per day!

funFacts.title: World's Heaviest Egg
funFacts.content: An ostrich laid the world's heaviest egg at over 2.5 lbs - that's equivalent to about 24 chicken eggs!

funFacts.title: Shell Color Myth
funFacts.content: Egg shell color is breed-dependent and has no impact on nutritional value. Brown eggs and white eggs are equally nutritious!

funFacts.title: Freshness Float Test
funFacts.content: Water flotation indicates egg age. Fresh eggs sink while older ones float due to the air cell inside expanding over time.

funFacts.title: Thick Whites Mean Fresh
funFacts.content: Egg white thickness correlates with freshness. Fresh eggs have thick, firm whites, while older eggs have thinner, more watery whites.

funFacts.title: Impressive Memory
funFacts.content: Chickens can recognize up to 100 faces, including humans! They remember who treats them well.

funFacts.title: Chickens Dream
funFacts.content: Chickens experience REM sleep and can dream, just like humans do. Wonder what they dream about?

funFacts.title: Diet Affects Yolk Color
funFacts.content: Diet quality affects yolk color and richness. Chickens with access to diverse greens and insects produce deeper orange yolks.

funFacts.title: Record-Breaking Hen
funFacts.content: One hen holds the record for laying 371 eggs in a single year. Talk about dedication!

funFacts.title: T-Rex Connection
funFacts.content: Genetic research reveals chickens share closer DNA with Tyrannosaurus rex than modern reptiles do. Your backyard dinosaurs!
